User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

Memory.title: Memory
Memory.description: Represents a memory for a specific date, sentence, and AI generated art.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Memory entity.

date.description: The date of the memory (one year prior).

sentence.description: The user-defined sentence associated with the memory.

aiArtUrl.description: URL of the AI-generated stick figure art.

UserMemoryReaction.title: UserMemoryReaction
UserMemoryReaction.description: Represents a user's reaction to a specific memory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserMemoryReaction entity.

reaction.description: The user's reaction to the memory (e.g., emoji).

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message within the journal/chat conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

userId.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatMessage) The user who sent the message.

memoryId.description: Reference to Memory. (Relationship: Memory 1:N ChatMessage) The memory that the chat message is associated with.

text.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the message was sent.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Uses path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores memories for each user. Uses path-based ownership; only the user can access their memories.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: memoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the memory.

definition.description: Stores reactions to a specific memory for a specific user. Uses path-based ownership; only the user can create, update, or delete their reactions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: memoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the memory.

params.name: reactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the reaction.

definition.description: Stores chat messages related to a specific memory for a specific user. Uses path-based ownership; only the user can create, update, or delete their messages.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: memoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the memory.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure data isolation and straightforward security rules by leveraging path-based ownership and denormalization. Each user has their own collection of memories and associated data, eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules. The `UserMemoryReaction` and `ChatMessage` subcollections are structured to enable easy querying and management of reactions and messages related to specific memories. Specifically, Authorization Independence is achieved by nesting data under `/users/{userId}`, where each user's data is isolated. The `UserMemoryReaction` and `ChatMessage` collections are nested under their respective `Memory` document, allowing for efficient queries and clear ownership. This design avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies, ensuring atomic operations.  The structure supports the required QAPs by using segregation and ownership models.
